creative_brief: Clean, minimal design with a single hero image of raw salmon on brown paper (unbranded, editorial feel). Muted earth tones, one accent-color CTA button. No stock photography — real texture, real food.
subject: I spent $47 on salmon and couldn't name the boat
preheader: That moment in the checkout line changed everything for me.
body:
I was standing in the checkout queue holding a $47 piece of salmon.

The label said "wild caught" and "sustainably sourced." I turned the packet over looking for something — anything — that told me where it actually came from. A boat name. A coastline. A person.

Nothing.

Forty-seven dollars, and I couldn't tell my daughter a single true thing about what we were about to eat for dinner.

That's the moment I stopped being frustrated and started building FarmThru.

I'm Rachel, and I want to tell you what we've done since that evening.

FarmThru is a grocery store — not a box scheme, not a weekend market — built on one rule: every product traces back to a real farm and a real name.

Collins Wild Caught lands their Alaskan salmon and seasonal Australian seafood off the South Coast of NSW. I can tell you the waters. Bundarra Farm raises the pork for their belly cuts and fennel salami. Paris Creek Farms in Meadows, SA, produces carbon-neutral biodynamic dairy. Farmer Brown supplies our eggs from NSW.

No middlemen. No wholesalers. No anonymous supply chains.

When a farm sells through us, they're paid fairly because there's nobody standing between their gate and your hands. And because we run a hub-and-collect model — you order online, then pick up from our Brookvale hub — we keep our costs honest without pretending home delivery is free.

This isn't luxury grocery shopping. It's grocery shopping that doesn't require you to lie to your kids when they ask where dinner came from.

We're opening FarmThru to everyday investors through equity crowdfunding on Birchal. You can join the waitlist now with a fully refundable $5 deposit to get early access before the campaign goes live.

I can't promise returns. I can't guarantee anything except that we'll keep doing what the big chains won't — showing you exactly where your food comes from.

If that $47 salmon moment sounds familiar, I'd love you to be part of what we're building.

This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice. Full details in the disclosure document on Birchal.

Rachel & the FarmThru team
